title: 6. Speech and Streaming
description: How Stream Mode, TTS backends, chunking, PocketTTS, and speech-related settings interact.

steps.title: Streaming Changes Timing
steps.body: Stream Mode lets speech begin before the full reply is complete. It feels more alive, but the setting should not be changed during an active generation.

steps.title: TTS Backend
steps.body: The TTS Backend chooses the installed speech engine. Chatterbox is the default; PocketTTS is isolated and may require Hugging Face terms and login for voice cloning.

steps.title: Chunking Basics
steps.body: Chunking controls how reply text is split into speech work. Smaller chunks can start faster; larger chunks can sound smoother. The defaults are meant to be safe.

steps.title: PocketTTS Login
steps.body: If PocketTTS cloning is installed but not ready, complete Hugging Face terms and login from the installer prompt or the PocketTTS runtime's hf command.

steps.title: Audio Devices
steps.body: Choose the correct input and output devices before troubleshooting speech. Device mismatch can look like a broken TTS path even when generation succeeded.
